arguments.task: Open official Plain sources and extract evidence for: product description, developer/API docs, authentication methods, pricing or access gating, API surface type and breadth, rate limits, and whether Plain documents an MCP server or Model Context Protocol integration. Prefer first-party pages only. Visit plain.com and any official subdomains linked from it such as docs or developers. Return the exact URLs visited and concise extracted facts with quotes where possible.

input.thought: Start a browser run on official Plain pages to gather first-party evidence for all required research fields.

raw_result.description: Customer support platform with shared inbox, workflow automation, and a GraphQL API for building support operations integrations.
raw_result.auth_notes: Plain's GraphQL authentication docs indicate API access via Machine Users that use bearer tokens/API keys for server-to-server requests; no first-party evidence was gathered for OAuth2, Basic, or separate API-key header auth.
raw_result.gating_reason: Plain's pricing page shows paid plans (Foundation, Horizon, Frontier) and no free developer tier was evidenced in the gathered first-party pages.

api_surface.breadth: Public GraphQL API documentation covers core support-domain resources and operations such as issues, customers, threads, attachments, and related workflows, indicating a substantive but product-scoped API surface.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Plain's docs navigation and GraphQL introduction evidence mention an MCP Server for Agents, indicating an existing MCP offering/documentation.

raw_result.blocker: Requires a paid Plain account and machine-user token setup before an agent toolkit can be built and tested.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No general API rate-limit policy was confirmed from the gathered first-party pages. Attachment docs referenced limitations, but broader rate limits were not evidenced.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs present a dedicated GraphQL endpoint, authentication guide, and an MCP-related docs entry, which are strong signals for agent-oriented extensibility.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The structured buildability_verdict is somewhat uncertain because public evidence gathered confirms a paid plan and token auth, but did not conclusively show whether API access is available on all paid tiers versus a narrower plan subset; general rate-limit evidence was also not found in the collected pages.
